items.title: Achtste finale Conference League: alle clubs en mogelijke affiches
items.summary: We zijn aangekomen in de achtste finale van de Conference League. Daarin zijn nog zestien clubs vertegenwoordigd, waaronder AZ, dat donderdag het Armeense FC Noah uitschakelde. Voetbal International schetst welke clubs door zijn én wat de affiches in de volgende ronde kunnen worden.

items.title: Deze clubs spelen in de achtste finales van de Europa League
items.summary: Alle zestien ploegen voor de achtste finales van de Europa League zijn bekend. Welke mogelijke affiches op de rol staan op donderdag 12 en 19 maart, lees je in dit overzicht.

items.title: Sacking Amorim could cost Man Utd nearly £16m
items.summary: Ruben Amorim's 14 months in charge of Manchester United have come at a price, with the club now paying up to £15.9m to sack him, in addition to £11m to hire the Portuguese.

items.title: Klopp's job at risk? 'Nonsense,' says Red Bull
items.summary: Red Bull backed former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp to remain the drinks giant's head of global soccer and dismissed a media report that his job could be under threat after barely a year.

items.title: Forest dodge fireworks before advancing in UEL
items.summary: Nottingham Forest players were forced to dodge fireworks thrown on to the pitch by Fenerbahce fans at the start of their Europa League playoff second leg.

items.title: Benfica deny that Prestianni admitted racist abuse
items.summary: Benfica have categorically denied that Gianluca Prestianni admitted to his teammates he racially abused Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: LaLiga will 'try again' to play league game in U.S.
items.summary: The Spanish soccer league wants to resurrect plans to stage a regular-season game in the United States.

items.title: Emotional Bunting wins in Belfast as Rock hits nine-darter and Littler loses again
items.summary: Rank-outsider Stephen Bunting was at his brilliant best to seal Premier League Darts victory on Night Four in Belfast as Josh Rock hit a nine-darter and Luke Littler suffered another early exit.

items.title: Nederland ook nog niet van België af in coëfficiëntenland
items.summary: Het Europese seizoen van Nederland is nog in leven nu AZ een comeback maakte tegen FC Noah , de Alkmaarders kunnen daardoor punten blijven pakken voor op de coëfficiëntenlijst.

items.title: AZ naar Praag of Athene in achtste finale Conference League
items.summary: AZ hoopte donderdagavond de achterstand tegen FC Noah weg te poetsen. De Alkmaarders verloren in Armenië met 1-0. Of dat lukte, lees je terug in deze VI Live.

items.title: Echteld geniet van liefhebber Smit bij AZ, maar wil hem vooral sparen
items.summary: Leeroy Echteld stond tevreden voor de camera na de 4-0 zege op FC Noah in de Conference League. Daarmee werd de blamage van vorige week in Armenië weggepoetst en kan AZ een kleurrijk einde van het seizoen tegemoet gaan.

items.title: Wigan demolish Leigh in blowout victory
items.summary: Wigan maintained their winning start to the new Betfred Super League season with a ruthless 54-0 derby demolition of Leigh.

items.title: Hudson-Odoi sends Nottingham Forest into last 16 despite fright by Fenerbahce
items.summary: For 67 minutes, Nottingham Forest were at risk of making unwanted history. At that point Fenerbahce were 2-0 up and full of belief that they could achieve the miracle their head coach Domenico Tedesco had been manifesting since their 3-0 first-leg defeat in Istanbul. Only one team in the history of European competition had lost the first leg of a tie by a three-goal margin at home and advanced. Up in the City Ground directors’ box, Evangelos Marinakis, more than an interested party on that occasion, presumably had flashbacks of Olympiakos suffering a 4-1 home defeat by Maccabi Tel Aviv in the Conference League two years ago, only to triumph 6-1 in Serbia in the second leg. Continue reading...

items.title: Forest survive Fenerbahce scare to seal EL last-16 place
items.summary: Nottingham Forest survived a scare from Fenerbahce to book their Europa League last-16 place, winning 4-2 on aggregate despite a 2-1 defeat at the City ground in Thursday's second leg.

items.title: Lacroix heads Crystal Palace past Zrinjski and into Conference League last 16
items.summary: It is a peculiarity particular to Crystal Palace that a season of such upheaval and unrest could still end up with Oliver Glasner’s side winning another trophy. Having left the pitch after last week’s first leg with supporters calling for the Austrian manager to be sacked in the morning, Maxence Lacroix and Evann Guessand made it a much more harmonious evening for Glasner and his side. After being fortunate to escape from last week’s trip to Bosnia and Herzegovina with a 1-1 draw, it could have been very different if Zrinjski had equalised just before Guessand settled the tie late on. But having been demoted from the Europa League to the Conference League after winning the FA Cup last season, Palace’s first-ever European campaign will continue against either Cypriot side Lanarca – who they lost to during the group stages – or Mainz from Germany in the last 16. Continue reading...

items.title: Guessand scores again as Glasner's Palace beat Zrinjski to earn last-16 spot
items.summary: Evann Guessand scored again late on as under-pressure Oliver Glasner watched his Crystal Palace side beat Zrinjski Mostar 2-0 at Selhurst Park to secure a 3-1 aggregate win in the UEFA Conference League and secure passage into the last 16.

items.title: Out of Europe - now Celtic face critical fortnight
items.summary: After exiting the Europa League, Celtic face a season-defining fortnight in the fight for the league and Scottish Cup.

items.title: RFU revamp of English rugby set to be confirmed
items.summary: A major overhaul of English rugby's top tier is set to be approved by the RFU on Friday, Sky Sports News understands.

items.title: Celtic save face in Stuttgart but Europa League miracle proves out of reach
items.summary: Martin O’Neill delivered a little piece of history in what will surely be his final European fixture as a manager. He created such wonderful memories for Celtic’s support in this domain during his first tenure that departing with a smile felt appropriate. In Stuttgart, Celtic won their first competitive game in Germany. It took them 17 attempts. “The evening is nothing to do with me,” said O’Neill with needless self-deprecation. Luke McCowan’s goal inside 30 seconds was irrelevant in the broader context of this tie. Stuttgart’s 4-1 canter in Glasgow a week earlier ensured that. Still, a game that had the whiff of irrelevance for Celtic delivered unexpected cheer. The statistics will show Stuttgart spent much of the evening camped in Celtic’s half – the hosts had 24 attempts at goal – but the Scottish champions played with a diligence and discipline that is worthy of huge credit. Sebastian Tounekti should even have delivered a second Celtic goal in the closing minutes. By then, Stuttgart were going through the motions. Continue reading...

items.title: Sutton's predictions v Oli Fox of Good Neighbours
items.summary: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on Oli Fox from indie band Good Neighbours, plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.
